title: Invite Yourself

meta.description: g0v Summit is centered on “Information Transparency, Open Results, and Open Collaboration,” we use the power of the grassroots to care for public affairs. In 2026, #🪑InviteYourself to Taipei to weave the future of civic space.

agenda.title: Agenda
agenda.info: The agenda is still being rolled out and is subject to change. Please check back for the most recent updates.
agenda.opass_info: You may find everything you need to know about the summit on the open-source OPass app (available on iOS & Android), where you can plan your schedule ahead of time and receive notifications as they’re about to start.

info.title: Traffic Info
info.venueName: Humanities & Social Sciences Building, Academia Sinica
info.address: 128 Academia Rd. Sec. 2, Nangang District, Taipei, Taiwan

publicTransportStep1.title: Reaching Nangang
publicTransportStep1.item1: Ride Taiwan Railway or Taiwan High Speed Rail to Nangang station.
publicTransportStep1.item2: Ride Taipei Metro to Nangang station (Bannan Line) or Nangang Exhibition Center station (Bannan Line & Wenhu Line).

publicTransportStep2.title: Reaching Academia Sinica
publicTransportStep2.item1: Summit Shuttle Bus operates daily from 7:50 to 9:30 between TRA Nangang Station ↔ North Entrance, HSSB, Academia Sinica with a 20-minute interval. Please keep an eye out for conference staff holding shuttle bus sign outside of the station and arrive early for an effortless ride to the venue.
publicTransportStep2.item2: From MRT Nangang station Exit 2, take route 212 Express, 270, or Blue 25 to Academia Sinica.
publicTransportStep2.item3: From MRT Nangang Exhibition Center station Exit 5, take route 205, 306, 620, 645, 679, S1, S5 or S12 to Academia Sinica.
publicTransportStep2.item4: Walk or ride YouBike to Humanities and Social Science Building.

drivingStep1.title: Driving Route
drivingStep1.item1: From National Highway No. 3, exit at Nanshen Rd., turn left onto Jiuzhuang St. Sec. 1, and then reach Academia Sinica on Academia Rd. Sec. 2.

drivingStep2.title: Vehicle Access
drivingStep2.item1: Drivers shall exchange a temporary parking permit at the entrance and park responsibly according to campus rules.

drivingStep3.title: Motorcycle Parking
drivingStep3.item1: As motorbikes are prohibited from entering the campus, please utilize free parking spaces next to Academia Sinica’s main entrance and side entrances.

cookieNotice: This website uses cookies to improve your experience. By continuing to use it, you consent to their use for advertising, social media, and analytics.

intro.title: Invite Yourself into a Braided Nexus
intro.p1: It’s often said that once bitten, twice shy. In times where democracy backsliding and social polarization are tearing the very fabric of civic space apart, activists may as well feel bitten by the venomous state of public dialogue.
intro.p2: Yet if we are tired of infuriating quarrels and political apathy, we shall move out from the comfort of armchairs and to braid back the society we desire ourselves. The decade-old g0v motto, hacktivism at its core, perfectly manifested the idea: “Ask not why nobody is doing this. You're that nobody—and ‘nobody’ can do everything.”
intro.p3: At g0v Summit 2026, we kindly ask you to “invite yourself” to take charge on braiding our future. Step into a three-dimensional civic space woven from different fabrics of the civic society—
intro.p4: Every participant, including you, represents a point in the coordinate system, defined by your unique skills, values, and stance. As we communicate with each other, points connect into lines. As countless lines intertwine, woven surfaces supported by dynamic network of action take shape, doubling as hammocks that gently catch us.
intro.p5: Thus, isolated nodes form clusters and single-threaded conversations evolve into collaborative ecosystems.
intro.p6: Derived from g0v’s core values: transparency, openness, and collaboration, we are braiding the Civic Coordinate System of g0v Summit 2026 with three major axes: (transparency–autonomy/privacy), (advocacy–recovery), (government agency–grassroots/civil society).
intro.p7: There are no outsiders here. Everyone can find their own coordinates to explore inward and connect outward.

venue.title: Venue
venue.name: Humanities and Social Sciences Building, Academia Sinica
venue.address: 128 Academia Rd. Sec. 2, Nangang District, Taipei, Taiwan

g0vSummit.title: What is g0v Summit?
g0vSummit.p1: Since 2012, under the call to “fork the government and code for society,” the g0v.tw (read as gov-zero) community has practiced over a decade of civic hacking. Centered on “Information Transparency, Open Results, and Open Collaboration,” we use the power of the grassroots to care for public affairs.
g0vSummit.p2: Following the Sunflower Movement, we launched the g0v Summit in 2014, building a bridge for interdisciplinary dialogue between open communities, government, NGOs, academia, and netizens.
g0vSummit.p3: Over these dozen years, Taiwanese society has weathered democratic crises, social turbulence, polarization, and “snake bites” of frustration.
g0vSummit.p4: Facing the scars of Taiwanese society and threats from near and far, “Ten Years” is not just a measure of time. It marks the continuous action and practice of the civic community: a process of transforming fear into resilience.
g0vSummit.p5: There is a saying: “Once bitten by a snake, ten years afraid of a straw rope.” But at g0v Summit 2026, we say: “Ten Years of Phah the Rope.” We believe in the power of the grassroots and our connection to one another. We can continuously transform that fear (Phah, to be afraid) into action (Phah, to beat/weave in Tâi-gí).
g0vSummit.p6: To Phah-kat (tie a knot in Tâi-gí) is not just about holding tight; it is about learning how to loosen. About loosening our passive posture, loosening dependency and fear. In an era of crisis and uncertainty, we are retraining our muscles for “Self Help” and “Mutual Aid.”
g0vSummit.p7: We refuse to recoil in passive fear of the rope. Instead, g0v Summit 2026 invites everyone here to roll up their sleeves, grab that straw rope, and start rubbing and beating it anew. As we branch out, weave, and connect, the “Snake” is no longer a phantom of terror, but a strong, organic community.

joinG0v.title: Join g0v
joinG0v.p1: g0v (gov-zero) is a grassroots social movement community dedicated to deepening the civic engagements and connections between the social arena and citizens. You can find like-minded partners in the g0v community, put your ideas into practice in a grassroots manner, and then release the results under an open-source license, letting people stand on your shoulders.
joinG0v.p2: g0v replaces the “o” in gov with “0”, thereby reimagining the role of government from scratch, and represents the vision from 0 to 1 of the generation of digital natives.

partners.title: Partners
partners.infolaw: Information Law Center, IIAS, Academia Sinica
partners.doit: Department of Information Technology, Taipei City Government

jothon.name: g0v Jothon
jothon.description: g0v Jothon is the task force responsible for organizing g0v’s Bi-monthly Hackathons and Infrathons, as well as managing the community’s fundraising affairs. Currently, the team consists of the Jothon Volunteer Group, project-based collaborators, and eight members of the Steering Committee.

friedrich.name: Friedrich Naumann Foundation For Freedom
friedrich.description:
The Friedrich Naumann Foundation For Freedom (FNF) is a German political foundation. In Germany and in 60 countries around the world, FNF and its partners promote freedom, liberalism, democracy, human rights, pluralism, tolerance, curbing climate change, an open and digital society, social market economy, and the rule of law. We are a non-profit organization that focuses on civic education.

We work with government institutions, NGOs, academia, political parties, researchers, students, journalists, activists, start-ups and artists who share our values and want to join us in promoting freedom. We work collaboratively and seek long-term partnerships, highlighting the importance of individual freedom and strong democratic institutions. FNF promotes economic freedom and free trade. We want businesses to respect human rights. We highly value privacy and data protection. FNF promotes curbing climate change by utilizing technological advances, for example in the context of smart cities.

FNF has worked in Asia since 1969. Today, we have five offices in Asia: Bangkok, Jarkata, Manila, Seoul, and Taipei. We also work in Malaysia.

In Taiwan, Taiwan Office/ Global Innovation Hub of the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom was established in 2021. The Hub focuses on “digital transformation” and “innovation for democracy.” With its international global network of experts, partners and collaborators, the hub aims to support the Friedrich Naumann Foundation on its way to becoming a creative innovation platform for liberalism.

luminate.name: Luminate
luminate.description: Luminate is a global foundation working to ensure that everyone – especially those who are underrepresented - has the information, rights, and power to influence the decisions that shape society. The foundation is focused on enabling people to fully participate in civic and political life, to safely challenge power, and to access accurate, trustworthy information. Luminate works globally with a regional focus on Africa, Asia, and Latin America. It was established by philanthropists Pierre and Pam Omidyar and has worked for over a decade on issues related to governance and citizen engagement.

support.title: Support g0v Summit
support.p1: g0v Summit is a volunteer-organized, non-profit event. “Invite Yourself” is our theme for 2026 — we invite everyone to pull up a stool and become part of the action. The sponsors listed above are the key supporters who make g0v Summit 2026 possible.
support.p2: Every g0v Summit depends on the support and participation of the community. Beyond attending sessions and events, you’re welcome to support the open community through sponsorship.
support.p3: We offer a variety of partnership packages including brand exposure, booth setup, ticket sponsorship, and more custom options. We look forward to working with you to advance civic technology.
support.p4: For sponsorship and partnership inquiries, please contact: sponsorship@summit.g0v.tw

ticket_info.title: How to Purchase Tickets
ticket_info.li1: Online Purchase: Click Get Tickets to purchase through the conference ticketing system (OEN).
ticket_info.li2: On-Site Cash Purchase: Bring cash (see the ticketing system for pricing) and visit the information desk during check-in hours on the first day of g0v Summit 2026 to purchase your ticket and check in on the spot.

howto.title: Check-In Guide
howto.p1: At g0v Summit, we've partnered with the Ministry of Digital Affairs (MoDA) to adopt the Digital Credential Wallet as the primary check-in method. The Digital Credential Wallet is one of the rare Taiwanese government projects that committed to open source as a procurement goal from the very beginning — with everything from servers to mobile apps released publicly. The volunteers of this year's g0v Summit share the spirit of Public Money, Public Code, and hope to collectively explore an identity verification process that is transparent, trustworthy, privacy-preserving, and supports selective disclosure. We welcome everyone to join us in this experiment! For clarity, "credential" below refers to a ticket stored in the TW DIW.

method1.title: Check-In with the TW DIW (Recommended; requires online purchase and a sufficiently recent Android or iOS device)
method1.li1: Download the Ministry of Digital Affairs' "Taiwan Digital Identity Wallet" app from the Google Play Store or iOS App Store and complete the initial setup.
method1.li2: When the pre-event email is sent, it will include a credential QR code link containing your ticket information. Scan or open the link using the TW DIW app to add your ticket credential to the wallet.
method1.li3: At the check-in venue, a verification QR code will be displayed. Scan it with the app to preview what credential information the conference is requesting from you.
method1.li4: Confirm and submit to complete check-in.

method2.title: Online Registration, Offline Check-In (requires online purchase)
method2.li1: To view your purchased ticket, follow these
method2.li2: Note down your ticket number (a sequence starting with "T", e.g., T123456789).
method2.li3: At the venue, visit the information desk and provide your ticket number to complete check-in.

method_cash.title: Cash Purchase Check-In
method_cash.p: Purchasing with cash on-site completes your check-in at the same time.

whatis.title: About the TW DIW
whatis.p1: What exactly is the TW DIW, and why use it?
whatis.p2: As governments worldwide increasingly move toward controlling digital identity, balancing basic identity verification with privacy protection on the internet is a challenge that both public and private sectors struggle with. If led entirely by governments, there's a risk of mass collection and surveillance of personal data; without any standards, companies facing verification needs might each develop their own tracking methods that are even more privacy-invasive.
whatis.p3: To reach a consensus, OpenID Foundation, W3C, and IETF have all had dedicated working groups in long-running discussions. Building on the vision of Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) and various Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) technologies, the community ultimately converged on Verifiable Credentials (VC) as a shared standard.
whatis.p4: The TW DIW developed by the Ministry of Digital Affairs is built on the OpenID4VC standard, establishing a three-party architecture of issuer — verifier — holder, all with open-source code. This means the issuing and verifying servers, as well as the app for storing credentials, can all be self-hosted by anyone without relying on a centrally managed database. We also hope this trial will help verify whether the project meets international standards, and potentially interoperate with other global implementations such as the EU's EUDI Wallet.
whatis.p5: Finally, on the second day of the Summit, there will be an open forum dedicated to discussing the TW DIW, as well as a workshop sharing the implementation process. Bring your questions and ideas — we'd love to have you there!

qa.q1: Will my data be sent to the Ministry of Digital Affairs? Is it safe?
qa.a1: Registration data is only accessible to volunteers with a need to know (including relevant members of the administration, logistics, and development teams). The source code for the issuing and verifying servers is provided by the Ministry of Digital Affairs, but the servers are set up and operated by g0v Summit volunteers on Summit-dedicated infrastructure — no data is sent to the Ministry of Digital Affairs. The data used to issue credentials comes solely from the conference ticketing system (OEN). Credential data is stored only on the g0v Summit server and within the app of users who choose to use the wallet. Check-in records are stored solely on the g0v Summit server and will be deleted after the conference. The servers and database are maintained by long-time g0v contributors who are professional software engineers, and we follow open-source best practices to ensure database security.
qa.q2: What data will the conference request from me?
qa.a2: The credentials issued through the TW DIW will only contain data that attendees have already provided through the conference ticketing system (OEN) — and not all of it. In accordance with the principle of data minimization, only the necessary information will be included in the credential and requested during verification. For example, using the selective disclosure feature of the TW DIW, we only need to request a field sufficient to prevent duplicate check-ins (such as your ticket number) — not the full contents of your credential. Before submitting your verification data, you can review what we're requesting in the app. For other use cases, such as picking up a bento box, the conference only needs to know your dietary preference (meat/vegetarian) and will not request any additional personal information.
qa.q3: Why don't you compile the app from source for attendees to install, or integrate it into OPass?
qa.q4: Do I have to check in using the TW DIW?
qa.a4: No. We understand that not everyone has a compatible device, or may have stricter privacy concerns. You can check in using just your ticket number and registration information at the venue. We even offer a cash purchase and check-in option. For those who purchase with cash on-site, your data will not pass through the conference ticketing system (OEN) or any of the conference's online systems at all. See the "Online Registration, Offline Check-In" and "On-Site Cash Purchase" sections above for details!
